No PDF was fetched (reading_mode is abstract_only, not full_text).

summaries.one_sentence: Based only on the abstract, Keshav's 2007 essay proposes a three-pass method for reading research papers (bird's-eye, content, reconstruction) but the full paper body has not been read in this run.
three_sentence: From the abstract alone: Keshav observes that researchers spend a great deal of time reading papers but few are taught how to read them effectively, and proposes a simple three-pass method to fill that gap.
The first pass is a quick bird's-eye view; the second pass gives a deeper understanding of the content; the third pass reconstructs the paper as if the reader were the author.
This run was performed in abstract_only mode — the full paper was not fetched, so the rest of this page is a preliminary read based only on the abstract, not a full three-pass reading.
ten_sentence: (The full body of the paper was not available at run time; sentences below are derived only from the abstract / opening paragraph and from common knowledge of the paper's reputation in CS pedagogy.)
Input was an abstract excerpt; input_kind = paper_excerpt; reading_mode = abstract_only.
From the abstract: the paper addresses the underexplored skill of reading research papers and proposes a three-pass method.
From the abstract: pass 1 is a quick bird's-eye view; pass 2 is a deeper read; pass 3 is a reconstructive read.
From the abstract: the method is intended for graduate students and researchers in computer science (per usual scope of the genre).
Specific time budgets (5–10 minutes, ~1 hour, 1–4 hours) are NOT confirmed in this run — they appear in the body of the paper, which was not fetched.
The Five Cs checklist (Category, Context, Correctness, Contributions, Clarity) is NOT confirmed in this run — it appears in the body of the paper, which was not fetched.
Detailed claims about reproduction / future work / limitations are NOT made in this run — the body is needed to ground them.
The page below shows the Five Cs as PRELIMINARY (based on the abstract), the Claims → Evidence map as derived only from the abstract, and Pass 2 / Pass 3 as 'unavailable — full text not obtained'.
Recommended next action: feed the full PDF or full text into this skill with the same paper title, then re-render with reading_mode = full_text.

main_ideas: [Author claim, from abstract] Reading research papers is a skill that is often taken for granted and rarely taught.
[Author claim, from abstract] A three-pass method can make paper reading more effective and more reproducible.
[Author claim, from abstract] Pass 1 = bird's-eye view; Pass 2 = deeper understanding; Pass 3 = reconstruction.
[Needs verification] Exact time budgets, exact Five Cs wording, exact list of references — all require the body to confirm.

glossary.definition: A reading procedure that splits one paper into three time-budgeted reads: bird's-eye, content, reconstruction. From the abstract of this paper.
A bird's-eye view of the paper. From the abstract. Time budget is in the body (not extracted in this run).
A deeper reading of the content. From the abstract. Time budget is in the body (not extracted in this run).
Reconstructing the paper as if the reader were the author. From the abstract. Time budget is in the body (not extracted in this run).
Evidence label used in this run for claims that are common-knowledge but not in the available text.
Evidence label used in this run for claims grounded in the abstract / opening paragraph only.
